items.title: The barcode project: a strategy to track the early naïve reservoir
items.summary: PI: Una T O'Doherty. Institution: EMORY UNIVERSITY. Award: $775,512. Memory CD4+ T cells are believed to be the primary reservoir for HIV; however, emerging evidence suggests an important role for naïve CD4+ T cells. Specifically, sequencing studies suggest the naïve reservoir contributes substantially to the intact HIV reservoir in chronic progressors, but is practically absent in elite controllers. While the naïve reservoir is small, it may have outsized effects on the overall reservoir since we find the naïve reservoir repopulates the memory based on preliminary clone tracking studies. Objective: The long-term goal is to dissect the role of the naïve reservoir on the overall reservoir in acutely infected individuals and in a primary model of latency. We will first determine the extent and frequency of naïve infection in acutely treated individuals. The n
items.why: HIV reservoir lab at Emory tracking naïve CD4+ T cells in lymph node/tissue; needs RNAscope for viral RNA localization where antibodies fail.

outreach_angle.hook: O'Doherty's lab is tracking HIV-infected naïve CD4+ T cells in tissue using clone barcoding and sequencing, but they'll need to validate viral RNA localization and cell-type identity in situ where antibodies are unreliable. RNAscope is the gold standard for detecting latent viral RNA and mapping infected naïve cells within lymphoid tissue architecture.
outreach_angle.way_in: Clone tracking tells you which cells are infected, but RNAscope can show you where those naïve CD4+ T cells sit in lymph node tissue and confirm active or latent HIV RNA in specific cell populations that flow cytometry alone cannot resolve. This bridges their sequencing findings back to tissue biology.
outreach_angle.routing: West territory, Emory-based immunology and virology focus. Route to rep covering academic medical centers in Atlanta area.

items.title: Epithelial Reprogramming Underpinning Euplastic versus Dysplastic Repair
items.summary: PI: Jaymin J Kathiriya. Institution: ICAHN SCHOOL OF MEDICINE AT MOUNT SINAI. Award: $873,801. PROJECT SUMMARY Lung possesses a remarkable ability to regenerate and restore normal lung function after severe acute injury. However, in the case of chronic injuries, the lung epithelial stem/progenitors fail to restore normal lung function and contribute to a pathologic regeneration, amounting to regenerative failure that typifies chronic restrictive lung diseases like Idiopathic Pulmonary Fibrosis (IPF). Both airway and alveolar epithelial stem cells are capable of differentiating into type 1 and type 2 alveolar epithelial cells (AT1 and AT2, respectively) via a series of intermediate cell states. During normal injury resolution, like in acute injuries in human lungs and murine models, both airway and alveolar epithelial stem cells go through a euplastic intermediate cell (EIC) state to
items.institution: ICAHN SCHOOL OF MEDICINE AT MOUNT SINAI
items.why: IPF lung epithelial differentiation with intermediate cell state validation; AT1/AT2 marker characterization needs in situ RNA localization.

outreach_angle.hook: Dr. Kathiriya's work maps epithelial stem cell differentiation through intermediate states during lung repair, requiring in situ validation of cell-type markers (AT1, AT2, and intermediate cells) within intact tissue architecture. This is a classic RNAscope use case: confirming single-cell RNA-seq or transcriptomic findings by localizing cell-state-specific transcripts directly in lung tissue sections.
outreach_angle.way_in: RNAscope multiplex panels can simultaneously detect AT1, AT2, and intermediate cell state markers in the same tissue section, revealing spatial relationships and transitional expression patterns that sequencing alone cannot provide. This enables definitive staging of the euplastic versus dysplastic repair pathway and validates marker genes without dependence on antibody availability.
outreach_angle.routing: NYA territory rep; lung/fibrosis biology focus fits Mount Sinai respiratory research cluster.

items.title: Harnessing microglia-neuron communication to improve habituation in neurodevelopment disorders
items.summary: PI: Jose H Ledo. Institution: MEDICAL UNIVERSITY OF SOUTH CAROLINA. Award: $226,500. This research proposal explores the role of microglial cells in neurodevelopmental and neuropsychiatric disorders, particularly focusing on habituation deficits induced by maternal immune activation (MIA). Novelty habituation, a fundamental form of behavioral plasticity where the response to a repeated stimulus diminishes over time, is often disrupted in conditions like autism spectrum disorders (ASD). The underlying brain circuits and cellular mechanisms responsible for these deficits remain poorly understood, posing a significant barrier to developing effective therapeutic interventions. Our study hypothesizes that MIA, a model for prenatal infection, shifts microglial cells from a homeostatic to a reactive state, impairing their ability to regulate neuronal activity and thus disrupting
items.why: Microglia and neuronal markers in ASD-relevant brain tissue; PI needs in situ validation of cell-type interactions and inflammatory state markers.

outreach_angle.hook: Dr. Ledo's work on microglia-neuron communication in maternal immune activation models requires validation of microglial activation state and neuronal response markers directly in brain tissue from habituation-relevant circuits. RNAscope is an ideal fit for a Neuroscience Cell-Type Marker Validator persona who needs to confirm which cells express inflammatory or homeostatic genes and map their spatial relationship to neurons.
outreach_angle.way_in: Maternal immune activation shifts microglia from homeostatic to reactive states, but standard immunostaining may miss low-abundance transcripts or lack specificity for activation-state markers without good antibodies. RNAscope can multiplex inflammatory cytokines, microglial markers, and neuronal genes in the same tissue section to spatially resolve the microglia-neuron interaction deficits driving habituation loss.

items.title: Thyroid hormone mediated reprogramming of the basolateral amygdala by adolescent social deprivation
items.summary: PI: Deena M. Walker. Institution: OREGON HEALTH & SCIENCE UNIVERSITY. Award: $625,866. PROJECT SUMMARY Social disconnection in humans, particularly during adolescence, is associated with substance use disorder vulnerability. In mice, adolescent social isolation profoundly alters reward processing in adulthood; increasing the preference for drugs of abuse, enhancing associative learning, altering dopaminergic signaling and transcription within the BLA and extended reward circuitry. Intriguingly, these phenomena are all also associated with thyroid hormone signaling. Further, dysregulation of thyroid hormone in early life is associated lifetime risk for reward-related mood disorders and altered development of monoaminergic systems. In adults, thyroid hormone dysregulation is associated with substance- and alcohol use disorders. Therefore, we propose that the persistent impacts
items.why: Neuro-psychiatric RNA validation in basolateral amygdala; social isolation and substance use models require in situ marker confirmation of dopamine and monoamine signaling changes.

They need to validate transcriptional changes and circuit-specific gene expression shifts in this small, functionally defined brain region where spatial context matters as much as cell type.
outreach_angle.way_in: RNAscope excels at confirming dopamine pathway markers and monoamine-related transcripts directly in BLA tissue sections, preserving the anatomical relationships that bulk sequencing or single-cell studies cannot capture. This is especially valuable for validating thyroid hormone-responsive genes and reward-circuit remodeling in their social isolation model without relying on antibody availability.

items.summary: PI: Shareef Shaheen. Institution: PENNSYLVANIA STATE UNIV HERSHEY MED CTR. Award: $37,108. PRORJECT ABSTRACT: JC Polyomavirus (JCPyV) is a ubiquitous asymptomatic virus that infects up to 90% of humans. In immunocompromised individuals, JCPyV can cause progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy (PML), an often-lethal demyelinating neurodegenerative disease. Because polyomaviruses (PyV) are species-specific, mechanisms of PML pathogenesis are incompletely understood. The Lukacher group studies Mouse Polyomavirus (MuPyV), a natural murine virus, to define virologic and immunologic factors involved in PyV CNS infection and disease. Published work from the Lukacher group has shown that Type I Interferons (IFNs) directly inhibit MuPyV replication and are necessary to mitigate MuPyV encephalitis. The pathway(s) of Type I IFN induction during PyV infection in vivo is unknown. In vitro,

items.title: Liquidity of gene co-expression trajectories across the lifespan highlights delayed maturation and the perinatal GABA switch in schizophrenia risk
items.summary: Schizophrenia genetic and environmental risk factors play out largely in early life, biasing development toward a pathogenic trajectory that becomes clinically apparent in early adulthood, when the disorder typically onsets. Here, we convert snapshots of gene expression in postmortem brain at a moment in time into a dynamic lifetime series employing the "liquidity" metric, a novel tool to track the evolution of networks across time from a multi-systemic perspective. The landscape of normal prefrontal cortical development becomes increasingly "liquid" during the first two decades of post-natal life, with sharp discontinuities in known critical periods such as birth and adolescence. Neurotypical individuals free of apparent neuropathology with relatively elevated polygenic risk scores for schizophrenia exhibit a generalized delay in the dynamics of liquidity across these trajectories compared to below-average-risk individuals. Impacted biological processes strongly converge on delayed GABA-A receptor functional maturation, involved in establishing Excitatory/Inhibitory balance in brain during early development. Similar to patients with schizophrenia, neurotypical high-risk individual
items.why: Psychiatric neuroscience lab (postmortem brain, schizophrenia cohort) validating GABAergic and developmental markers in prefrontal cortex; RNAscope ideal for spatial circuit and cell-type marker confirmation.

outreach_angle.hook: This lab is mapping developmental gene co-expression trajectories in postmortem prefrontal cortex across the lifespan, with a focus on GABAergic maturation and schizophrenia risk. They need spatial validation of their computational findings, especially GABA-A receptor expression and cell-type markers in specific cortical layers and circuits where developmental timing matters.
outreach_angle.way_in: RNAscope multiplex assays can confirm GABA-A receptor and GABAergic marker expression at single-cell resolution within defined prefrontal cortical regions and laminae, bridging their bulk transcriptomics discoveries back to the tissue architecture and developmental timing that drives E/I balance disruption in high-risk individuals.
outreach_angle.routing: National territory, psychiatric/developmental neuroscience focus, postmortem brain expertise.

items.title: Organization of Retinothalamic Circuits and their Contributions to Visual Processing in Primary Visual Cortex
items.summary: PI: EDWARD M CALLAWAY, Benjamin Knoell Stafford. Institution: SALK INSTITUTE FOR BIOLOGICAL STUDIES. Award: $475,000. Project Summary/Abstract The goal of this proposal is to characterize the connectivity and functional role of specific circuits in the mammalian visual system. This proposal focuses on neural circuits that transmit visual information from retinal ganglion cells (RGCs) in the retina, through different regions of the dorsal lateral geniculate nucleus (dLGN) in the thalamus, to specific populations of neurons in the primary visual cortex (V1). The combined activity of these visual pathway circuits results in the generation of tuning properties in V1 neurons (eg. direction selectivity) that make important contributions to visual perception and behavior. The objective of this proposal is to understand how specific retino-geniculo-cortical circuits, carrying different types of visual information
items.pi: EDWARD M CALLAWAY, Benjamin Knoell Stafford

items.title: Schwann Cell Reprogramming after Nerve Injury
items.summary: PI: Rajeshwar B Awatramani, John P Svaren. Institution: UNIVERSITY OF WISCONSIN-MADISON. Award: $475,401. Abstract Myelination of axons in the nervous system is critical for not only conduction of action potentials, but also for providing tropic support to ensure long term survival of neurons in both the central and peripheral nervous systems. Myelin disorders are a major cause of neurological disease, and can be caused by genetic disorders, infectious disease, and inflammation. The peripheral nervous system has substantial plasticity in being able to regenerate after nerve injury, and critical transcription factors and their target gene networks have begun to be elucidated. Schwann cell reprogramming to a new differentiated state is a critical and rate limiting factor in peripheral nerve regeneration, particularly when regeneration is impaired as a function of aging/chronic denervation. There

items.title: Blood-Brain Barrier Repair in Alzheimer’s Disease with Epilepsy
items.summary: PI: Bjoern Bauer, Anika M.S. Hartz. Institution: UNIVERSITY OF KENTUCKY. Award: $719,390. More than 25% of patients with Alzheimer’s disease (AD) develop epilepsy as co-morbidity. In AD with epilepsy (ADxEpi), seizures accelerate cognitive decline and further reduce life expectancy compared to AD alone. One hallmark of both AD and epilepsy is blood-brain barrier dysfunction. We discovered that barrier dysfunction is more severe in ADxEpi patients compared to seizure-free AD patients. Collectively, our data suggest that a combination of Ab and seizure-released glutamate (Aβ/Glu) triggers a dual positive feedback loop which exac- erbates barrier dysfunction, seizures, and cognitive decline in ADxEpi. However, the detailed mechanism(s) that leads to barrier dysfunction in ADxEpi is/are unknown, and treatment options for ADxEpi patients are limited to anti-seizure drugs that by the
items.why: BBB dysfunction in AD+epilepsy requires cellular/molecular validation in brain tissue; microglia and endothelial markers ideal for RNAscope.

outreach_angle.hook: This lab is studying blood-brain barrier dysfunction in Alzheimer's disease with comorbid epilepsy, a condition driven by Aβ and glutamate-triggered mechanisms in brain tissue. They will need to validate cell-type-specific markers (endothelial cells, microglia, pericytes) and map regional BBB integrity changes across brain tissue, making them a strong fit for in situ RNA validation.
outreach_angle.way_in: RNAscope can localize barrier-relevant transcripts (tight junction proteins, inflammatory cytokines, endothelial markers) directly in their brain tissue samples and correlate expression with BBB dysfunction regions, giving them spatial context that bulk sequencing or immunostaining alone cannot provide.
outreach_angle.routing: RBA (East) field sales; University of Kentucky, Neuroscience/CNS focus.

items.title: Maintenance of mucosal homeostasis by commensal Th17 cells
items.summary: PI: Ivaylo Ivanov Ivanov. Institution: COLUMBIA UNIVERSITY HEALTH SCIENCES. Award: $567,000. Mucosa are sites of conventional immune system attack for defense against invasive pathogens. Mucosa were also once regarded simply as inert barriers preventing commensal microbes from accessing sterile tissues. Instead, emerging evidence suggests a complex interplay between commensal microbes, barrier epithelial cells, and the immune system that is essential for local and systemic homeostasis of the host. We have recently provided insight into how recognition of commensal components by epithelial and innate immune cells triggers a specific immune reaction and why the nature of this reaction could foster host- microbe symbiosis instead of outright microbial rejection. Specifically, we showed that antigen acquisition be intestinal epithelial cells (IECs) of commensal antigens drives a non-p
items.why: Barrier-organ inflammation lab (gut epithelium, Th17, commensal immunity). Needs spatial validation of cytokine/marker localization in mucosal tissue sections.

outreach_angle.hook: Ivanov's lab studies how commensal bacteria and epithelial cells interact to maintain mucosal homeostasis through Th17 cell responses, requiring precise mapping of cytokine and immune marker expression across intact gut tissue. RNAscope is the ideal tool to validate Th17-specific transcripts and low-abundance cytokine RNA (like IL-17) directly in tissue sections where conventional antibodies often fail.
outreach_angle.way_in: When validating Th17 differentiation and cytokine production in mucosal tissue, antibodies against secreted proteins (IL-17, IL-22) are unreliable. RNAscope fluorescent or chromogenic ISH lets the lab detect the actual mRNA in epithelial and immune compartments simultaneously, confirming which cells are responding and where in the tissue architecture.
outreach_angle.routing: NYA territory. Immunology/cell biology profile suggests fit with spatial inflammation expertise.

items.title: Single-cell transcriptomics of heterogeneous patient-derived organoids reveals novel therapeutic targets in high-grade serous ovarian cancer
items.summary: High-grade serous ovarian carcinoma (HGSOC) is characterized by widespread peritoneal dissemination and poor long-term survival, largely driven by metastatic relapse following initial response to chemotherapy. Defining the molecular programs that enable tumor progression from the primary ovarian site to metastatic niches remains a key challenge. Here, we leverage patient-derived organoids (PDOs) coupled with single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) to interrogate tumor evolution and identify regulators of metastatic competence in HGSOC. We profiled PDOs and matched formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded (FFPE) tumor samples from ovarian and omental disease sites across seven patients. Single-cell transcriptomic analysis revealed conserved and patient-specific cellular states and enabled reconstruction of inferred trajectories of tumor progression. Comparative trajectory analysis identified gene expression programs associated with metastatic transition from ovarian to omental tumors. Among these, the heparan sulfate proteoglycan AGRIN emerged as a consistently upregulated gene along the metastatic axis. items.why: PDO scRNA-seq lab studying ovarian cancer metastasis needs in situ validation of trajectory-derived markers in FFPE tissue; strong RNAscope prospect for spatial subtype and state mapping.

outreach_angle.hook: This lab is profiling single-cell transcriptomics across patient-derived organoids and FFPE ovarian cancer tissue to map tumor progression and metastatic competence. They have identified gene expression programs and candidate drivers (like AGRIN) along the metastatic axis that now need spatial validation in the original tissue context.
outreach_angle.way_in: RNAscope can validate their scRNA-seq-derived markers and trajectory states directly in FFPE tumor samples, answering where in the tissue these metastatic gene programs are active and whether AGRIN and other candidates show the expected spatial distribution across ovarian versus omental disease sites.
outreach_angle.routing: National territory; lead with spatial transcriptomics frame for single-cell validation in primary tissue.

items.title: Molecular Instruments Announces Fully Ambient-Temperature HCR™ Pro RNA-ISH on the BOND-III, Setting a New Standard for Clinical Automation
items.summary: Molecular Instruments press release via newswire.
items.why: Molecular Instruments (HCR) launches fully ambient-temperature automation on BOND-III. Direct head-to-head threat on speed, cost, and ease vs. RNAscope. High competitive urgency.

impact_brief.what_happened: Molecular Instruments announced that its HCR (Hybridization Chain Reaction) RNA ISH assay now runs fully at ambient temperature on the Leica BOND-III automated stainer, eliminating heating steps. This moves HCR closer to a turnkey, standardized clinical workflow that competes directly with RNAscope's manual and semi-automated deployment models.
impact_brief.why_it_matters: This is a direct competitive strike on ACD's core market. HCR on BOND-III combines Leica's installed base in clinical labs, full automation, and simplified temperature control into a single integrated offering. For customers already using BOND-III (or considering it), this becomes a platform-lock bundle that bypasses RNAscope's flexibility advantage. Speed and ease-of-use are becoming table stakes in clinical ISH; ambient temperature removes a friction point that may have favored RNAscope in field conversations.
at_risk: Clinical lab accounts evaluating or using BOND-III stainers now face a single-vendor automation story that reduces incentive to adopt RNAscope as a standalone reagent.
ACD's selling narrative around platform agnosticity and avoiding lock-in weakens if HCR becomes the default ISH on BOND-III in key verticals (e.g., pathology, oncology panels).
Ambient-temperature assays lower technical barriers for labs, potentially expanding HCR's addressable market faster than RNAscope can scale automation partnerships.
holding_ground: RNAscope's single-molecule sensitivity and specificity remain unmatched in published head-to-heads; ambient temperature does not change HCR's fundamental detection performance relative to RNAscope.
ACD's deep validation across diverse tissue types and disease applications (lung, breast, neurology, etc.) provides credibility that a newly automated assay format must still earn in clinical use.

RNAscope's platform-agnostic design means it can integrate with multiple stainers (BOND, Ventana, etc.) and retain flexibility; a single BOND-III lock is not inevitable if ACD builds competitive automation partnerships or keeps manual workflows attractive.

items.title: Molecular Instruments and Abcam Announce Broad Compatibility of the HCR™ HiFi Encoder with Abcam’s Best-in-Class RabMAb® and Mouse Antibody Portfolio
items.summary: Molecular Instruments press release via newswire.
items.why: Molecular Instruments expands HCR ecosystem via Abcam antibody compatibility, strengthening head-to-head positioning against RNAscope on speed, cost, and workflow integration.

impact_brief.why_it_matters: HCR is RNAscope's primary direct competitor in multiplexed ISH. By tightening integration with a major antibody vendor, Molecular Instruments is building ecosystem stickiness and lowering switching costs for customers evaluating platforms. This move targets labs that already use Abcam antibodies and can now do RNA and protein detection in a single, streamlined workflow without format switching or validation risk.
at_risk: Customer acquisition cost and switching friction may decrease for labs already embedded in the Abcam ecosystem, as HCR becomes a plug-and-play addition to their existing reagent stack.

Mid-market academic and contract research labs that value ease-of-use and bundled solutions may perceive lower total friction with HCR plus Abcam versus RNAscope plus separate antibody sourcing.
holding_ground: RNAscope's single-molecule sensitivity and specificity remain unmatched by HCR and are backed by deeper publication and tissue-type validation, which matters for rigorous research and diagnostics.

RNAscope is platform-agnostic and works across any fluorescence microscope, whereas HCR ecosystem partnerships may create subtle pressure to adopt compatible detection hardware, limiting customer freedom.

ACD's direct relationships with antibody vendors and established compatibility protocols (e.g., with major commercial antibody lines) provide equivalent convenience without lock-in risk, and can be strengthened through similar visibility and partnership announcements.

at_risk: HCR gains credibility and freedom to operate in head-to-head sales conversations, especially in accounts evaluating alternatives to RNAscope.

Momentum and narrative: the press release positions HCR as a validated, legally safe option, which may accelerate interest from risk-averse customers and collaborators.
holding_ground: RNAscope's single-molecule sensitivity and deep cross-tissue validation track record remain the core competitive differentiator; legal victory does not change assay performance or clinical/research adoption history.

Platform agnosticity-RNAscope works across customer instruments and workflows without ecosystem lock-in-is still a structural advantage that HCR cannot claim if bundled tightly with Molecular Instruments' own platform or partners.
Remaining patent portfolio and other IP claims may still provide tactical leverage; this ruling covers two specific patents but does not eliminate all IP risk vectors.

impact_brief.what_happened: Vizgen closed a $48 million financing round led by Arch Venture Partners, M Ventures, and Northpond Ventures. The company plans to use the capital to scale commercial operations globally, accelerate custom content turnaround times, improve margins, and expand customer engagement, with CEO Rob Carson (who joined after the Ultivue merger in 2024) stating the goal is a path to profitability.
impact_brief.why_it_matters: Vizgen is a direct spatial transcriptomics competitor with MERSCOPE platform capabilities overlapping RNAscope use cases. Fresh, multi-lead institutional backing signals investor confidence in the spatial biology market and Vizgen's ability to compete, while explicit focus on commercial scaling, global reach, and margin optimization suggests Vizgen will intensify sales and customer acquisition efforts in the near term.
at_risk: Vizgen's capital position now enables aggressive sales hiring and channel expansion in markets where ACD and Vizgen overlap, particularly in research and early clinical adoption.
Emphasis on turnaround time for custom content and global customer engagement could appeal to labs frustrated with current RNAscope service timelines or seeking alternative platforms.
Post-Ultivue merger, Vizgen owns immunofluorescence IP and may bundle spatial transcriptomics with multiplex IF, creating a single-vendor workflow advantage for some customers.
holding_ground: RNAscope's single-molecule sensitivity and deep validation track record across diverse tissue types remain differentiated from transcriptomics-only platforms like MERSCOPE.

RNAscope's platform-agnostic design means customers are not locked into Vizgen or any single instrument ecosystem, preserving flexibility that capital-backed scaling of a closed platform cannot replicate.

Vizgen's need to pursue profitability suggests margin pressure and unresolved unit economics, whereas ACD can compete on established product maturity and customer retention rather than aggressive customer acquisition burn.

items.title: mRNA Nuclear Export Machinery as Target of SARS-CoV-2 Nsp1 Protein
items.summary: PI: Beatriz MA Fontoura, Adolfo Garcia-Sastre, Yi Ren. Institution: VANDERBILT UNIVERSITY. Award: $802,444. The global COVID-19 pandemic caused by the SARS-CoV-2 outbreak underscores the need for understanding viral-host interactions that impact host gene expression and antiviral responses. A critical virulence factor of coronaviruses is the nonstructural protein 1 (Nsp1). We uncovered a previously unknown mechanism of Nsp1- mediated inhibition of host antiviral responses: Nsp1 targets the cellular mRNA nuclear export pathway. Nuclear export of cellular mRNAs through the nuclear pore complex (NPC) is obligatory for eukaryotic gene expression including those genes encoding antiviral factors. By contrast, SARS-CoV-2 mRNA synthesis takes place at viral transcriptional factories in the cytoplasm. We have demonstrated that SARS-CoV-2 Nsp1 protein inhibits host mRNA nuclear export by targeting the cel
items.pi: Beatriz MA Fontoura, Adolfo Garcia-Sastre, Yi Ren
items.why: Vanderbilt virology lab studying SARS-CoV-2 host-pathogen interactions; will need in situ viral RNA and host gene localization validation in infected tissue.

outreach_angle.hook: This lab is validating viral-host interactions at the subcellular level, specifically how SARS-CoV-2 Nsp1 disrupts mRNA export machinery in infected cells and tissue. They will need to localize both viral RNA and host antiviral transcripts within infected tissue contexts to confirm their mechanistic findings.
outreach_angle.way_in: RNAscope can simultaneously detect SARS-CoV-2 genomic and subgenomic RNA alongside host mRNA targets (NPC components, antiviral genes) in the same infected tissue section, providing single-cell spatial context that complementary approaches like Western blots or qPCR cannot. This multiplex capability is ideal for mapping which host transcripts are blocked from export in Nsp1-expressing cells versus bystander cells.
outreach_angle.routing: GCA territory (West). Assign to rep covering Vanderbilt virology and infectious disease research groups.

items.title: Fenwick Represents Treeline Biosciences in Definitive Merger Agreement with Standard BioTools - fenwick.com
items.summary: Fenwick Represents Treeline Biosciences in Definitive Merger Agreement with Standard BioTools fenwick.com
items.why: Standard BioTools (Hyperion IMC, spatial proteomics) acquires Treeline; consolidates spatial/imaging portfolio but RNA focus remains secondary to proteomics.

impact_brief.what_happened: Standard BioTools has acquired Treeline Biosciences under a definitive merger agreement. This consolidates Standard BioTools' spatial imaging portfolio, which already includes Hyperion IMC for spatial proteomics, and strengthens its position in the broader spatial biology market.
impact_brief.why_it_matters: Standard BioTools is building an integrated spatial proteomics and imaging ecosystem around Hyperion IMC. While the acquisition does not directly target RNA, it signals competitive consolidation in spatial biology and reinforces Standard BioTools' strategy of platform bundling and instrument-centric lock-in, a known competitive threat to open-architecture tools like RNAscope.
at_risk: Customers seeking bundled spatial workflows (protein + RNA imaging) may perceive Standard BioTools as a more complete single-vendor solution after Treeline integration.
Standard BioTools' proteomics-first narrative could frame RNA as a secondary modality in spatial analysis, reducing RNAscope positioning in labs that want unified vendor relationships.
holding_ground: RNAscope remains unmatched in single-molecule RNA sensitivity and specificity, a core need that Treeline's acquisition does not directly address.
ACD's platform independence means customers are not locked into Standard BioTools' instruments, preserving flexibility for labs that need RNAscope on their existing or preferred imaging hardware.
Standard BioTools' proteomics focus leaves RNA still at the periphery of their strategic narrative, creating space for ACD to own the RNA spatial biology conversation.

items.title: Vizgen Announces $48 Million Financing
items.summary: Waltham, Mass. – January 8, 2026 – Vizgen, Inc., a leader in spatial multiomics, today announced the closing of a $48 million financing. The financing was led by ARCH Venture Partners and co-led by M Ventures... The post Vizgen Announces $48 Million Financing appeared first on Vizgen .
items.why: Vizgen (MERSCOPE) raises $48M; direct spatial transcriptomics competitor strengthening market position and R&D capacity.

impact_brief.what_happened: Vizgen closed a $48 million Series funding round led by ARCH Venture Partners, with participation from M Ventures (Merck KGaA's venture arm) and existing investors. The company plans to use proceeds for R&D roadmap execution, global sales expansion, and manufacturing scale-up, and has already shipped MERFISH 2.0 chemistry and expanded proteomics offerings (InSituPlex, STARVUE) since late 2024.
impact_brief.why_it_matters: Vizgen is a direct spatial transcriptomics competitor with a locked-in instrument platform (MERSCOPE) and is now well-capitalized to accelerate innovation, sales, and manufacturing. The involvement of Merck KGaA's corporate VC signals both strategic validation and potential for deeper industry partnerships. ACD's platform-agnostic advantage is also its vulnerability: customers who choose MERSCOPE become locked into Vizgen's ecosystem and chemistry, reducing TAM for RNAscope in those accounts.
at_risk: Account acquisition in spatial transcriptomics where MERSCOPE is positioned as the integrated workflow; Vizgen's capital and manufacturing scale will enable faster platform adoption and price competition.
R&D velocity in spatial transcriptomics chemistry and analysis (MERFISH 2.0, STARVUE) may outpace ACD's ability to defend feature parity without significant internal investment.
Enterprise/pharma relationships, especially if Merck KGaA leverages its strategic stake to bundle MERSCOPE into drug discovery workflows or preferred vendor agreements.
holding_ground: RNAscope's single-molecule sensitivity and specificity remain scientifically differentiated and are agnostic to instrument vendor, giving customers flexibility Vizgen's bundled platform does not.
Deep, multi-tissue validation library and open compatibility with third-party imaging systems lock in researchers who need portability and do not want ecosystem lock-in.

Vizgen's path to profitability and stated focus on manufacturing scale suggests near-term capital will go to production and sales, not radical chemistry innovation that would force ACD to react immediately.

items.title: Looking Ahead to 2026: A Vizgen View from Our CEO
items.summary: Dear Vizgen Family, At Vizgen, 2025 was one of both significant evolution and steadfastness. We bolstered our protein and co-detection capabilities, successfully resolved our prior litigation and introduced new, class-leading chemistry. At the same time, we moved – as always, with speed –... The post Looking Ahead to 2026: A Vizgen View from Our CEO appeared first on Vizgen .
items.why: Vizgen (MERSCOPE) strengthening protein detection and chemistry; direct head-to-head spatial multiplexing competitor advancing capabilities.

impact_brief.what_happened: Vizgen announced significant new capital funding and highlighted 2025 achievements including bolstered protein and co-detection capabilities, resolved litigation, and new chemistry. The CEO letter emphasizes MERFISH 2.0 capabilities, expanded ADC markers, and lab services, alongside a website refresh positioning the company for 2026 growth.
impact_brief.why_it_matters: Vizgen is a direct competitor in spatial multiplexing and in situ detection. Fresh capital and claimed chemistry advances signal aggressive R&D investment and market positioning. Protein detection improvements and co-detection capabilities directly address the same tissue analysis use cases where RNAscope operates, particularly in oncology and neuroscience applications.
at_risk: Vizgen's protein detection and co-detection gains may narrow capability gaps in multiplex workflows that currently favor RNAscope's flexibility and single-molecule sensitivity.
Well-capitalized competitor can outspend on field support, lab services, and marketing, especially among institutions already committed to MERSCOPE instruments.
MERFISH 2.0 positioning and expanded marker panels suggest Vizgen is iterating rapidly; continued feature parity erosion is possible if ACD does not maintain innovation pace.
holding_ground: RNAscope's platform-agnostic design remains a structural advantage; customers using Vizgen instruments cannot easily switch detection chemistry, whereas RNAscope users retain instrument optionality.

Deep, long-standing validation across diverse tissue types and disease models is difficult to replicate; Vizgen's advances in protein detection do not automatically override established RNAscope trust in RNA specificity and sensitivity.

Lab services and support claims require operational delivery; RNAscope's embedded expertise in tissue biology and assay troubleshooting remain a defensible moat if actively leveraged in customer engagement.

items.title: Vizgen to Debut Spatial Multiomics Roadmap at AGBT 2026
items.summary: Vizgen will expand applications of MERSCOPE Ultra™ platform to include spatially-resolved molecular profiling of organoids; company has expansive pipeline and is progressing toward multiple breakthrough capabilities Waltham, Mass. – February 19, 2026 – Vizgen, Inc., a leader in spatial multiomics at single-cell resolution, today announced a series of innovations under development to help accelerate drug discovery, advance understanding of disease progression, and enable new research breakthroughs. The company will present its... The post Vizgen to Debut Spatial Multiomics Roadmap at AGBT 2026 appeared first on Vizgen .
items.why: Vizgen expanding MERSCOPE Ultra to organoids; direct competitor pipeline broadening into RNAscope-adjacent market segments.

impact_brief.what_happened: Vizgen announced a spatial multiomics roadmap for AGBT 2026 that expands MERSCOPE Ultra into organoid profiling, adds modular customization (Add-On Genes), improves slide chemistry and pre-analytical workflow, and is developing 3D imaging, cloud-based bioinformatics, and advanced segmentation. The company is positioning itself around regulatory interest in NAMs (non-animal models) and human-relevant tissue systems.
impact_brief.why_it_matters: Vizgen is systematizing and broadening its platform's application range in ways that directly overlap RNAscope's core markets: multiplexed RNA detection in complex tissues and organoid models. Their roadmap emphasizes ecosystem completeness (slides, chemistry, panels, bioinformatics, 3D) that could increase platform stickiness and reduce customer motivation to adopt best-of-breed alternatives like RNAscope for specific use cases.
at_risk: Organoid profiling and NAM workflows are emerging research segments where RNAscope has validation strength but Vizgen is now packaging end-to-end solutions with regulatory alignment.
Modular Add-On Genes and expanded predesigned panels make MERSCOPE more flexible and hypothesis-friendly, reducing perceived need for orthogonal or alternative ISH platforms.
Cloud bioinformatics and 3D imaging roadmap suggest Vizgen is moving upstream and downstream to capture more of the customer workflow and lock in instrument usage.
holding_ground: RNAscope's single-molecule sensitivity and cross-tissue validation remain unmatched; organoid work does not change the fundamental performance advantage in tissues where multiplexing density and specificity matter most.

Platform agnosticity is a structural ACD strength Vizgen cannot replicate: RNAscope works on any imaging system, whereas MERSCOPE's value depends on the instrument ecosystem, giving customers flexibility to switch or diversify.

RNAscope's deep literature footprint and adoption in translational oncology, neurology, and pathology are established; Vizgen's organoid pivot is new and will require clinical validation and adoption cycles to prove commercial traction.

items.title: Casdin funds deepen tie to Standard BioTools (LAB) with 23.9% stake and Treeline merger support - Stock Titan
items.summary: Casdin funds deepen tie to Standard BioTools (LAB) with 23.9% stake and Treeline merger support Stock Titan
items.why: Standard BioTools (Hyperion imaging mass cytometry) strengthens via Casdin capital and Treeline merger; spatial proteomics competitive pressure, adjacent to RNAscope's spatial footprint.

impact_brief.what_happened: Casdin Capital has increased its stake in Standard BioTools to 23.9% and is supporting a merger with Treeline Biosciences. This deepens Casdin's control over Standard BioTools (maker of Hyperion imaging mass cytometry) and likely accelerates product development and market expansion in spatial proteomics.
impact_brief.why_it_matters: Standard BioTools' strengthened capital position and merger with Treeline could accelerate adoption of multiplexed spatial proteomics as a complementary or alternative modality to spatial transcriptomics. This expands competitive pressure in the broader spatial biology market where ACD competes with RNAscope, even though proteomics and transcriptomics target different biological questions.
at_risk: Customers evaluating spatial biology platforms may bundle proteomics and transcriptomics workflows, potentially favoring integrated vendor solutions over best-in-class point products.
Well-capitalized Standard BioTools may invest heavily in sales, partnerships, and go-to-market in geographic or vertical segments where ACD has market presence.
Treeline's assets (if complementary to Hyperion) could create a broader spatial omics narrative that positions Standard BioTools as a multi-modal player.
holding_ground: RNAscope's single-molecule sensitivity and deep validation across tissue types remain unmatched in spatial transcriptomics, and proteomics alone does not measure RNA expression.
ACD's platform-agnostic design avoids lock-in and allows customers to pair RNAscope with any downstream analysis or imaging platform, preserving optionality that bundled competitors cannot match.
Hyperion is an imaging mass cytometry tool (protein detection), orthogonal rather than directly competitive to RNAscope; this capital round does not introduce a new transcriptomic rival.

items.title: 10x Genomics acquires Proteintech Genomics division By Investing.com - Investing.com Australia
items.summary: 10x Genomics acquires Proteintech Genomics division By Investing.com Investing.com Australia
items.why: 10x expands into antibody/protein reagents via Proteintech acquisition. Monitor for bundled spatial protein + RNA offers competing with RNAscope + antibody workflows.

impact_brief.what_happened: 10x Genomics has acquired Proteintech's Genomics division, bringing antibody and protein reagent capabilities in-house. This move extends 10x's portfolio beyond spatial transcriptomics into the protein detection reagent space.
impact_brief.why_it_matters: 10x can now bundle spatial RNA (Visium) with validated antibodies and protein assays, creating an integrated workflow that reduces friction for customers doing multiplexed RNA and protein analysis. This tightens ecosystem lock-in and positions 10x to offer simpler go-to-market packages than point solutions like RNAscope plus third-party antibodies.
at_risk: Customers performing simultaneous spatial RNA and protein quantification may face pressure to adopt 10x's bundled offering rather than mixing RNAscope with third-party reagents.
10x can market coordinated optimization and technical support across RNA capture and protein detection, reducing perceived need for independent ISH platforms.
Proteintech antibodies gain preferential positioning in 10x protocols and documentation, potentially marginalizing alternative protein detection approaches.
holding_ground: RNAscope single-molecule sensitivity and specificity remain superior to spatial transcriptomics for fine-grained RNA localization and quantification.
RNAscope's platform-agnostic design allows customers to use any downstream protein assay (10x antibodies, third-party, or other) without lock-in to one vendor's instrument.
RNAscope has deep, published validation across diverse tissue types and disease models that bundled offerings cannot quickly replicate at scale.
